---
title: 'Are gas prices affected by the sitting US President (Under Construction, testing html view)'
date: '2024-09-03T20:47:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Topos Creative, LLC'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1917584311154797185'
blogger_filename: '/2024/09/are-gas-prices-affected-by-sitting-us.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Topos Creative, LLC/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Data Analysis'
---

<h1><a name="gas-prices-in-usa-historical-analysis">Gas Prices in USA, historical
analysis<o:p></o:p></a></h1>

<p class="FirstParagraph">This
report is intended to review gas prices in the USA historically for comparison
against various claims.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBodyText">One
such claim is that the sitting US President has a direct affect on gas prices.<o:p></o:p></p>

<div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;">

<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" />

</div>

<h2><a name="X31d71eb68e27fe22eef5b63fadb11d0b54b6017">Data from the EIA - US Energy
Information Administration<o:p></o:p></a></h2>

<p class="FirstParagraph">This dataframe
set <span class="VerbatimChar"><span style="font-size: 11pt;">GasPrices_eia_prices_1970_2022</span></span>
comes from the EIA website as a downloadable CSV.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBodyText">The <a href="https://www.eia.gov/TOOLS/FAQS/faq.php?id=26&amp;t=10">EIA</a> provides an FAQ
for using the data, which includes instructions to download the <a href="https://www.eia.gov/state/seds/seds-data-complete.php?sid=US#CompleteDataFile">CSV</a> and for a
reference <a href="https://www.eia.gov/totalenergy/data/monthly/index.php#appendices">Excel</a> document that
helps with conversion.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBlockText">â€œTo obtain the
historical prices from the SEDS data, use the CSV file for All Statesâ€”Prices.
In the file, the code for gasoline prices for the transportation sector, in <span class="VerbatimChar"><span style="font-size: 11pt;">$/MMBtu</span></span>, is: State
Abbreviation (in column A) and <span class="VerbatimChar"><span style="font-size: 11pt;">MGACD</span></span> (in column B). For
example, the code for Alaska is <span class="VerbatimChar"><span style="font-size: 11pt;">AKâ€”MGACD</span></span>. Those prices, in
$/MMBtu, can be converted to approximate dollars per gallon using the heat
contents in Table A3 Petroleum consumption and fuel ethanol. There are 42 U.S.
gallons in a barrel.â€<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="FirstParagraph">The CSV is
downloaded from &gt; Section: Prices and expenditures &gt; Subsection: All
price and expenditure estimates &gt; SubSubsection: Prices &gt; 1970-2022 &gt;
XLSX CSV ZIP<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBodyText">The following
shows the barrel prices falling sharply well before 2016â€™s national election,
and remaining relatively stable until after the pandemic crashes of 2020.<o:p></o:p></p>


<pre><code>
<p class="SourceCode">```{python}</p><p class="SourceCode"><span class="ImportTok">import</span><span class="NormalTok"> pandas </span><span class="ImportTok">as</span><span class="NormalTok"> pd</span></p><p class="SourceCode">
<span class="ImportTok">import</span><span class="NormalTok">
matplotlib.pyplot </span><span class="ImportTok">as</span><span class="NormalTok">
plt</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Path to the CSV file</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">file_path </span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok"> </span><span class="StringTok">'C:/Users/dwolfe/Documents/Demo_Skills_SampleCode/Projects/National_Market_Markers/_dataframes/GasPrices_eia_prices_1970_2022.csv'</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Read the CSV file</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">df </span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok"> pd.read_csv(file_path)</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Filter rows where 'MSN' column contains 'MGACD'</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">filtered_df </span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok"> df[df[</span><span class="StringTok">'MSN'</span><span class="NormalTok">].</span><span class="BuiltInTok">str</span><span class="NormalTok">.contains(</span><span class="StringTok">'MGACD'</span><span class="NormalTok">, case</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="VariableTok">False</span><span class="NormalTok">, na</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="VariableTok">False</span><span class="NormalTok">)]</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Calculate the average for each year for the
filtered data</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">average_prices </span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok"> filtered_df.iloc[:, </span><span class="DecValTok">3</span><span class="NormalTok">:].mean()&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Assumes year data starts at the 4th column</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Create a line plot</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.figure(figsize</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok">(</span><span class="DecValTok">10</span><span class="NormalTok">, </span><span class="DecValTok">5</span><span class="NormalTok">))&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Set the
figure size</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.plot(average_prices.index,
average_prices.values, marker</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="StringTok">'o'</span><span class="NormalTok">)&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Plot the
average prices</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.title(</span><span class="StringTok">'Average
Gas Prices Over Years for MGACD'</span><span class="NormalTok">)&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Add a title</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.xlabel(</span><span class="StringTok">'Year'</span><span class="NormalTok">)&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Add x-axis label</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.ylabel(</span><span class="StringTok">'Average
Price'</span><span class="NormalTok">)&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Add y-axis label</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.grid(</span><span class="VariableTok">True</span><span class="NormalTok">)&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Add a grid</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.xticks(rotation</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="DecValTok">45</span><span class="NormalTok">)&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Rotate x-axis
labels for better readability</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.tight_layout()&nbsp;
</span><span class="CommentTok"># Adjust layout to make room for the
rotated x-axis labels</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.show()&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Display the plot</span><o:p></o:p></p><p class="SourceCode"><span class="CommentTok">```</span></p>
</code>
</pre>

<div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/eia-a5ed64334671.png" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="277" data-original-width="560" height="316" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/eia-ff5c7ad9b739.png" width="640" /></a></div><br /><p class="FirstParagraph"><br /></p>

<div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;">

<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" />

</div>



<h2><a name="data-from-our-world-in-data">Data from Our World in Data<o:p></o:p></a></h2>

<p class="FirstParagraph">Our World in Data provides an
international dataset to review.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBodyText">This dataframe set <span class="VerbatimChar"><span style="font-size: 11pt;">GasePrices_OWD_crude-oil-prices.csv</span></span> comes from the
Our World in Data website as a downloadable CSV.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBlockText">â€œData Page: Oil price - Crude
prices since 1861â€, part of the following publication: Hannah Ritchie, Pablo
Rosado and Max Roser (2023) - â€œEnergyâ€. Data adapted from Energy Institute.
Retrieved from https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/crude-oil-prices [online
resource]<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="FirstParagraph">These international crude oil
prices show the same trends, clearly indicating that the US President is
unrelated to gas prices.<o:p></o:p></p><p class="FirstParagraph"><br /></p>

<pre><code>

<p class="SourceCode">```{python}</p><div><span class="ImportTok">import</span><span class="NormalTok"> pandas </span><span class="ImportTok">as</span><span class="NormalTok"> pd</span></div><p class="SourceCode">
<span class="ImportTok">import</span><span class="NormalTok">
matplotlib.pyplot </span><span class="ImportTok">as</span><span class="NormalTok">
plt</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Path to the CSV file</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">file_path </span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok"> </span><span class="StringTok">'C:/Users/dwolfe/Documents/Demo_Skills_SampleCode/Projects/National_Market_Markers/_dataframes/GasPrices_owd_intl_1861_2023.csv'</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Read the CSV file</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">df </span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok"> pd.read_csv(file_path)</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Display the first few rows to verify the data</span><br />
<span class="BuiltInTok">print</span><span class="NormalTok">(df.head())</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Filter data for years 1970 to 2023</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">filtered_df </span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok"> df[(df[</span><span class="StringTok">'Year'</span><span class="NormalTok">] </span><span class="OperatorTok">&gt;=</span><span class="NormalTok"> </span><span class="DecValTok">1970</span><span class="NormalTok">)
</span><span class="OperatorTok">&amp;</span><span class="NormalTok"> (df[</span><span class="StringTok">'Year'</span><span class="NormalTok">] </span><span class="OperatorTok">&lt;=</span><span class="NormalTok"> </span><span class="DecValTok">2023</span><span class="NormalTok">)]</span><br />
<br />
<span class="CommentTok"># Plotting the filtered data</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.figure(figsize</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="NormalTok">(</span><span class="DecValTok">12</span><span class="NormalTok">, </span><span class="DecValTok">6</span><span class="NormalTok">))&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Set figure
size</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.plot(filtered_df[</span><span class="StringTok">'Year'</span><span class="NormalTok">], filtered_df[</span><span class="StringTok">'Oil price - Crude
prices since 1861 (current US$)'</span><span class="NormalTok">], marker</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="StringTok">'o'</span><span class="NormalTok">, linestyle</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="StringTok">'-'</span><span class="NormalTok">)</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.title(</span><span class="StringTok">'International
Crude Oil Prices from 1970 to 2023'</span><span class="NormalTok">)</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.xlabel(</span><span class="StringTok">'Year'</span><span class="NormalTok">)</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.ylabel(</span><span class="StringTok">'Oil Price
(current US$)'</span><span class="NormalTok">)</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.grid(</span><span class="VariableTok">True</span><span class="NormalTok">)</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.xticks(rotation</span><span class="OperatorTok">=</span><span class="DecValTok">45</span><span class="NormalTok">)&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Rotate x-axis
labels for better readability</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.tight_layout()&nbsp;
</span><span class="CommentTok"># Adjust layout</span><br />
<span class="NormalTok">plt.show()&nbsp; </span><span class="CommentTok"># Display the plot</span><o:p></o:p></p>


<p class="SourceCode"><span class="VerbatimChar">&nbsp;
Entity&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Code&nbsp; Year&nbsp;
Oil price - Crude prices since 1861 (current US$)</span><br />
<span class="VerbatimChar">0&nbsp; World&nbsp; OWID_WRL&nbsp;
1861&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
3.082002</span><br />
<span class="VerbatimChar">1&nbsp; World&nbsp; OWID_WRL&nbsp;
1862&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
6.604290</span><br />
<span class="VerbatimChar">2&nbsp; World&nbsp; OWID_WRL&nbsp;
1863&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
19.812870</span><br />
<span class="VerbatimChar">3&nbsp; World&nbsp; OWID_WRL&nbsp;
1864&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
50.695790</span><br />
<span class="VerbatimChar">4&nbsp; World&nbsp; OWID_WRL&nbsp;
1865&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
41.449783</span><o:p></o:p></p><p class="SourceCode"><span class="VerbatimChar">```</span></p>

</code>
</pre>



<div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/oqd-33a2ef9b7b62.png" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="278" data-original-width="560" height="318" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/oqd-ca5b67d68bcd.png" width="640" /></a></div><br /><p class="FirstParagraph"><br /></p>

<div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;">

<hr align="center" size="2" width="100%" />

</div>



<h2><a name="placeholder-tbc">Placeholder TBCâ€¦<o:p></o:p></a></h2>

<p class="FirstParagraph">Text<o:p></o:p></p>
